item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those just-for-the-fun-of-it SCiPs that I just love to read. It's entertaining. Would have liked to see it be more vague, & the last audio kind of ruined any mystery that it would have otherwise had: It contrasted the article before it to a degree. That being said, I love this.

Random thought, is the title at all related to (& I understand this makes absolutely no sense) the Justin Timberlake Album Futuresex/Lovesounds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very cool concept. Interview with former employee was hilarious! Love the title as well. A play on FutureSex/LoveSounds? Brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't want to upvote, until I read the logs. Well done on those, sir

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i almost wanted to upvote for the skip name alone ("FutureSkip/LuxeShop"… yes good) and it kept my interest but my problem was with the last log. I looked at the old log with the current one, but I personally just think it should be natched altogether. +45 shows that I'm wrong, but eh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it a lot!
totpd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually thought the over the top pop-culture-future stuff in the last addendum was charming as hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels similar enough to, and not quite as good as, that interdimensional zoo concessions stand that I don't think I'm going to upvote. I will say that the long interview was a little… long? And you definitely don't need the paragraph describing all the employees in detail. It could definitely be condensed. I will say that the various fashion items were intriguing and varied, at least, practical while still being weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Huh. Was "Class Blue" perchance a reference to the GOC's classification system? Specifically, that Class Blue means a healer or other biologically-manipulative being? Because that seems like a clever euphemism for "You're a lifesaver". :)

item.title: This is not really a migrant store, is it.
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, my gene donor used to tell me you don't really register what you have until it's gone.

… word, my batch-bro. Word. + 1

item.title: Re: My functionary
item.content:encoded:
It was deleted and the deletion was announced here:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-785679/deletions-21:up-up-down-down-left-right-left-right-b-a

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've fixed the last log. I'm a lot happier with how it turned out not, if y'all will take another look at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this one a lot. I thought that the crazy luxury items were clever, the weirdly-similar but not quite employees were a nice touch, and overall it was something kind of unusual while still fitting into the mainlist landscape. I was all set to upvote.

Then I hit the logs.

The first one was sort of irritating, but I stayed my urge to downvote; it did convey the whole information-gathering side of the skip, so it did have a reason to be there. I didn't care for the "hey man" tone of the person being interviewed, but I suppose there are people who do talk like that, though it all still came off as fairly unnatural.

The second one, as people above have said, just kills this. Silly pulp magazine time traveling gangsters with a tendency to reveal all of the stuff they're doing in tidy, concise conversations. Any mystery that the skip had was neatly excised and replaced with a cheeseball future. It wrecked the reading experience to the point where I had to downvote.

item.title: Re: My functionary
item.content:encoded:
Was that the fourteen year old in containment that would set himself and others aflame? That was deleted after getting too many down votes.

Anyway, I like what you did here. The idea that time and space can be traveled by a few thugs is interesting, but I agree that you should drop that entire last log. The mystery would definitely add to the article, spilling the beans like you did takes away a lot of the fun.

item.title: Re: My functionary
item.content:encoded: SCP slots are reused when the initial article is deleted, if that's what your asking.

item.title: Re: My functionary
item.content:encoded: Most likely was deleted.

item.title: My functionary
item.content:encoded: My opinion on this particular SCP is questionable. I HAD a an SCP called "The Flame boy" in this SCP-2426 slot. May you consider an explanation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, dropping signature words from classic science fiction into the time travelers' lexicon is way too corny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right, that was the function of the entire last addendum. :P I wanted to juxtapose the grand power it must take to destroy the barrier of time with a small time crime ring. I'll remove the addendum once I figure out how to more subtly show the money laundering side.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should put something in the description about how it manifests in malls and such. Other than that, it's pretty nice, although it's pretty similar to SCP-1141. The last Addendum felt a bit shoehorned in to explain the twist to stupid people (like me). I think it'd be much better if you just dropped a few more subtle hints in the Interview. No vote for now, but that could easily change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP, but fuck the whole last log. It tells everything that you just painstakingly showed so elegantly. Get rid of that and you've got my axe upvote.

item.title: its good... but
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, I like it
BUT

  • you still have an "SCP-XXXX" in there toward the bottom of the description, might wanna change that
  • I wish you had mentioned that it manifests in several locations in the beginning of the description rather than it being thrown in later, granted you put it in the special containment procedures but since it's one of the SCPs main anomolous properties it should be described in the description
  • finally, (my opinion) I'm not a fan of that recorded sequence at the end at all. I feel like it really takes away from the mysteriousness that this SCP has by basically telling us they are salesmen from the future (I think)

Overall, I really do like the SCP, but a it could be ironed out just a little
